claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm inquiring about my child Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're inquiring about Alex, who is 6 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are some specific circumstances where you may still be able to claim Child Benefit.

**Which country are you currently living in, and are you or your partner a Crown servant (such as a diplomatic service employee or member of HM Forces posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in France at the moment. No, I'm not a Crown servant - neither I nor my partner work for the diplomatic service or are in the armed forces.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since you're living in France and are not a Crown servant, I need to understand your situation a bit better.

**Are you an EEA or Swiss national, or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme? Or are there any other ties you maintain with the UK (such as paying UK taxes or National Insurance)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any of those. I'm not an EEA or Swiss national, I don't have settled or pre-settled status, and I'm not paying UK taxes or National Insurance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, I need to make my assessment:

- Alex is 6 years old (under 16, so meets the age requirement)
- You're living in France
- You're not a Crown servant
- You don't have EEA/Swiss national status, settled/pre-settled status, or UK tax/National Insurance ties

According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 **and you live in the UK**. While there are exceptions for people living abroad (such as Crown servants or those living in certain countries with specific arrangements), you've indicated that none of these exceptions apply to your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

While Alex meets the age requirement (being 6 years old and under 16), the main issue is the residency requirement. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. There are exceptions for people living abroad, but unfortunately none of these apply to your situation:

- You're not a Crown servant (diplomat or armed forces member posted abroad)
- You don't have EEA/Swiss national status or settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You're not paying UK taxes or National Insurance

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you would then be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex at that time.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant is living in France and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow someone living abroad to claim Child Benefit. The claimant is not a Crown servant, does not have EEA/Swiss national status, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not paying UK taxes or National Insurance. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. Since the claimant does not live in the UK and does not qualify under any of the specific exceptions for those living abroad, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is living in France with their 6-year-old child Alex. While Alex meets the age requirement for Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement of living in the UK, nor do they qualify under any of the exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit (such as being a Crown servant or having specific EEA/EU Settlement Scheme status). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. Since the claimant does not live in the UK and does not qualify under any of the specific exceptions for those living abroad, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

While Alex meets the age requirement for Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement of living in the UK, nor do they qualify under any of the exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit (such as being a Crown servant or having specific EEA/EU Settlement Scheme status). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
